1: For his mercy endureth endureth for ever.
2: Who Who can shew forth all his praise?
3: And And he that doeth righteousness at all times.
4: O visit me with thy salvation;
5: That I may rejoice in the gladness of thy nation, that I may glory with thine inheritance.
6: We have committed iniquity, we have done wickedly.
7: But provoked him him at the sea, even even at the Red sea.
8: That he might make his mighty power to be known.
9: So he led them through the depths, as through the wilderness.
10: And redeemed them from the hand of the enemy.
11: There was not one of them left.
13: They waited not for his counsel:
14: And tempted God in the desert.
15: But sent leanness into their soul.
16: And And Aaron the saint of the LORD LORD .
17: And covered the company of Abiram.
18: The flame burned up the wicked.
20: Into the similitude of an ox that eateth grass.
21: Which had done great things in Egypt;
22: And And terrible things by the Red sea.
23: To turn away his wrath, lest he should destroy them them .
25: And And hearkened not unto the voice of the LORD LORD .
26: To overthrow them in the wilderness:
27: And to scatter them in the lands.
28: And ate the sacrifices of the dead.
29: And the plague brake in upon them.
30: And so so the plague was stayed.
32: So that it went ill with Moses for their sakes:
33: So that he spake unadvisedly with his lips.
34: Concerning whom the LORD LORD commanded them:
35: the heathen, and learned their works.
36: And they served their idols: Which were a snare unto them.
37: Yea, they sacrificed their sons and their daughters unto devils,
38: Whom they sacrificed unto the idols of Canaan: And the land was polluted with blood.
39: And went a whoring with their own inventions.
40: Insomuch that he abhorred his own inheritance.
41: And they that hated them ruled over them.
42: And they were brought into subjection under their hand.
43: But they provoked him him with their counsel, and were brought low for their iniquity.
45: And he remembered for them his covenant, and repented according to the multitude of his mercies.
46: He made them also to be pitied of all those that carried them captives.
47: To give thanks unto thy holy name, and and to triumph in thy praise.
